inputs: 16kHz mono WAV bytes, duration >= 1.0s — multipart form field 'file' or JSON body {"audio_base64": ...}
Authorization: Bearer ${VOICEPRINT_SERVICE_TOKEN} header, required unless ALLOW_UNAUTHENTICATED=true is explicitly set
outputs: 192-dimensional float embedding vector (list[float]), cosine-comparable across calls and across enrolled voiceprints stored by meeting-api (issue #27 speaker_profiles/voiceprints tables)
duration_seconds: float — decoded audio duration as measured by the service
model: fixed string "ecapa-tdnn" identifying the embedding model used
evidence: services/voiceprint-service/tests/ (17 unit tests, model mocked — no real download in CI)
services/voiceprint-service/tests/test_golden_fixture.py (proves /embed plumbing is deterministic for a fixed mocked model output)

boundaries: no third-party network egress at inference time — the model runs locally in-container, matching the PII policy's 'local processing only' requirement (audio and embeddings never leave the deployment)

the service does not persist audio bytes or embeddings anywhere (no disk, no DB, no cache beyond the in-memory request lifetime) — storage, consent recording, and encryption are the caller's responsibility (meeting-api voiceprints/voiceprint_consents tables, issue #27)
the service never logs embedding vector values or raw audio/base64 bytes at any log level (enforced by services/voiceprint-service/tests/test_no_pii_in_logs.py)
returns 503 for /embed when VOICEPRINT_SERVICE_TOKEN is unset and ALLOW_UNAUTHENTICATED is not explicitly enabled — refuses to silently run an unauthenticated biometric endpoint
the service performs no matching, thresholding, or identity decisions — it only turns audio into a vector; cosine comparison and the VOICEPRINT_SUGGEST_THRESHOLD decision happen in meeting-api
forbidden_claims: embeddings from this endpoint are a legal or biometric identity proof

cosine similarity across two calls to this endpoint is guaranteed above a specific accuracy bound (FMR/FRR) — no benchmark has been run against this deployment; auto-apply thresholds are out of scope for this phase (suggest-only)
the service enforces retention, consent, or deletion — those are meeting-api invariants (DB-level FK constraints + sweeps), not this service's responsibility

checks: /embed rejects payloads larger than VOICEPRINT_MAX_AUDIO_BYTES with 413 (test_embed_rejects_payload_over_max_bytes)
/embed requires a valid Bearer token when VOICEPRINT_SERVICE_TOKEN is configured, 401 on missing/mismatched token (test_auth.py)
/embed returns 503 when no token is configured and ALLOW_UNAUTHENTICATED is unset (test_embed_returns_503_when_token_unset_and_unauthenticated_not_allowed)
/embed returns 503 while the model has not finished loading (test_embed_returns_503_when_model_not_loaded)

/embed returns 429 once MAX_ACTIVE_REQUESTS in-flight requests are exceeded, with Retry-After header, and releases the slot after completion (test_embed_returns_429_when_concurrency_limit_exceeded, test_embed_slot_is_released_after_request_completes)
/health reports status=loading (503) before warmup completes and status=healthy (200) after (test_health.py)

golden fixture: a fixed input + fixed mocked-model output reproduces byte-identical response shape and values through the real /embed request pipeline (test_golden_fixture.py) — this proves plumbing determinism, not real-model determinism (no real ECAPA-TDNN inference runs in CI)
log records emitted during /embed never contain the returned embedding values or the request's audio/base64 bytes (test_no_pii_in_logs.py)
